message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-011 in src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py
What: discover_and_confirm type-guards the confirmer return (non-Verdict -> FirewallViolation).
Test(s): test_discover_and_confirm_rejects_non_verdict_confirmer
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
2eea958 fix(hardening-p4): batch C — provenance, observability & resample memory (G-009,010,012,023)

d61bd4e fix(hardening-p4): batch A — methodology integrity (G-001,005,006,011,017)
---
src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:10:hand (same risk Firewall A had before ADR-0008). :func:`discover_and_confirm` makes it
src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:33:from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:39:from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:45:    def __call__(self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, held_out: Corpus) -> Verdict: ...

src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:160:            raise FirewallViolation(

120	    if not held_out.units:
121	        # the confirmation partition selects no actual corpus units — confirming on an empty
122	        # held-out set is a vacuous test (the split ids don't match the corpus). Fail loud.
123	        raise FirewallViolation(
124	            "confirmation partition selects no corpus units — nothing to confirm on "
125	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
126	        )
127	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
128	    # within-run hypothesis uniqueness (pass 3, #F-018): a ``discover`` step returning the same
129	    # hypothesis_id twice would append two verdicts for one hypothesis, inflating the verdict
130	    # count. The pipeline runner guards this via ``_require_unique_ids``; mirror it in the
131	    # public standalone runner so both entry points enforce the same identity invariant.
132	    seen_hypothesis_ids: set[str] = set()
133	    for hypothesis in hypotheses:
134	        if hypothesis.hypothesis_id in seen_hypothesis_ids:
135	            raise FirewallViolation(
136	                f"duplicate hypothesis_id {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} returned by discover() — "
137	                "each discovered hypothesis must have a unique id"
138	            )
139	        seen_hypothesis_ids.add(hypothesis.hypothesis_id)
140	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
141	            raise FirewallViolation(
142	                f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is {hypothesis.origin.value!r}; the "
143	                "discovery runner expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
144	            )
145	        # Pre-registration enforced by the runner: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content
146	        # (no post-lock content swap) and its attested lock time must precede this confirmation.
147	        require_preregistered(
148	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
149	        )
150	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)
151	        # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing verdict.hypothesis_id would
152	        # raise an opaque AttributeError instead of a typed firewall error (pass 4, #G-011 —
153	        # mirrors the pipeline/adjudication guards from #F-021).
154	        if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
155	            raise FirewallViolation(
156	                f"confirm returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
157	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
158	            )
159	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
160	            raise FirewallViolation(
161	                f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for hypothesis "
162	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
163	            )
164	        verdicts.append(verdict)
165	    return verdicts
166

1	"""Discovery runner — Firewall B (discover/confirm separation) enforced by construction.
2	
3	The tests ARE the enforcement: the discover step must be handed only the discovery partition,
4	the confirm step only the held-out partition, and misuse (non-discovery / unlocked hypothesis,
5	misattributed verdict) must be structurally caught.
6	"""
7	
8	import datetime as _dt
9	
10	import pytest
11	
12	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus, Relation, Unit
13	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import discover_and_confirm, subset_corpus
14	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
15	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
16	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import LocalHmacAuthority, lock_hypothesis
17	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict, VerdictLabel
18	
19	# Real pre-registration authority for the tests: discovered hypotheses must be genuinely locked
20	# (the runner verifies the content-binding proof and lock-before-confirm ordering).
21	_AUTH = LocalHmacAuthority(b"discovery-test-secret-key-000001")
22	_LOCK_INSTANT = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc) - _dt.timedelta(hours=1)
23	
24	
25	def _corpus():
26	    return Corpus(
27	        units=tuple(Unit(f"u{i}", f"t{i}") for i in range(6)),
28	        relations=(Relation("u0", "u1", "r"), Relation("u0", "u3", "cross")),
29	        # a per-unit index in corpus metadata is exactly the Firewall-B leak vector:
30	        # if subset_corpus carried this through, the discovery step would see the held-out units.
31	        metadata={"by_unit": {f"u{i}": f"secret-{i}" for i in range(6)}},
32	    )
33	
34	
35	def _split():
36	    return DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"u0", "u1", "u2"}, {"u3", "u4", "u5"})
37	
38	
39	def _locked_discovery(hid="H1"):
40	    return lock_hypothesis(
41	        Hypothesis(
42	            hypothesis_id=hid,
43	            statement="pattern",
44	            kind=HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL,
45	            origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
46	            test_name="t",
47	            decision_rule="r",
48	        ),
49	        authority=_AUTH,
50	        instant=_LOCK_INSTANT,
51	    )
52	
53	
54	# ---- discover_and_confirm: duplicate + empty guards (pass 3) ----
55	
56	
57	def _confirm_ok(hypothesis, held_out):
58	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, "r", statistic=0.0, threshold=0.05)
59	
60	
61	def test_discover_and_confirm_rejects_duplicate_hypothesis_ids():
62	    # #F-018: a discover step returning the same hypothesis_id twice would append two verdicts
63	    # for one hypothesis, inflating the verdict count. The standalone runner must guard it like
64	    # the pipeline's _require_unique_ids does.
65	    dup = _locked_discovery("H-dup")
66	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="duplicate hypothesis_id"):
67	        discover_and_confirm(
68	            _corpus(),
69	            _split(),
70	            discover=lambda c: [dup, dup],
71	            confirm=_confirm_ok,
72	            authority=_AUTH,
73	        )
74	
75	
76	def test_discover_and_confirm_rejects_non_verdict_confirmer():
77	    # #G-011: a confirmer returning a non-Verdict (e.g. None — forgot to return) must raise a typed
78	    # FirewallViolation, not an opaque AttributeError at verdict.hypothesis_id (mirrors #F-021).
79	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"):
80	        discover_and_confirm(
81	            _corpus(),
82	            _split(),
83	            discover=lambda c: [_locked_discovery("H1")],
84	            confirm=lambda h, held_out: None,
85	            authority=_AUTH,
86	        )
87	
88	
89	def test_discover_and_confirm_rejects_empty_discovery():
90	    # #F-042: discover() returning nothing is a vacuous run indistinguishable from a broken
91	    # callable that forgot to return — fail loud rather than complete with zero verdicts.
92	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="no hypotheses"):
93	        discover_and_confirm(
94	            _corpus(),
95	            _split(),
96	            discover=lambda c: [],
97	            confirm=_confirm_ok,
98	            authority=_AUTH,
99	        )
100	
101	
102	# ---- subset_corpus ----
103	
104	
105	def test_subset_keeps_only_partition_units_and_internal_relations():
106	    sub = subset_corpus(_corpus(), {"u0", "u1", "u2"})
107	    assert {u.unit_id for u in sub.units} == {"u0", "u1", "u2"}
108	    # the u0->u1 relation is internal (kept); the u0->u3 cross-partition relation is dropped
109	    assert [(r.source_id, r.target_id) for r in sub.relations] == [("u0", "u1")]
110	
111	
112	def test_subset_drops_corpus_metadata():
113	    # corpus metadata can hold a per-unit index covering the WHOLE corpus; carrying it into a
114	    # subset would leak the held-out partition into the discovery step. It must be dropped.
115	    sub = subset_corpus(_corpus(), {"u0", "u1", "u2"})
116	    assert not sub.metadata  # empty/falsy — the foreign-unit index is gone
117	
118	
119	# ---- Firewall B by construction ----
120

content.text:
The test discriminates perfectly: with the fix reverted it fails with the exact opaque `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` that G-011 was meant to prevent (the test expects a `FirewallViolation` matching "expected a Verdict"). With the fix in place, all 12 tests pass and the file is cleanly restored.

Assessment:
- Appropriate: The guard uses `isinstance(verdict, Verdict)` immediately after the confirmer call, raising a typed `FirewallViolation` with the type name and hypothesis id. It's placed correctly before the `verdict.hypothesis_id` misattribution check, so the None case is caught first. Idiomatic, consistent with the surrounding `FirewallViolation` guards, and mirrors the documented #F-021 pipeline/adjudication pattern. No regression (full file's 12 tests pass).
- Complete: It fully closes the gap (any non-Verdict return, including None, is rejected), and the named test genuinely discriminates.

input.reasoning:
Fix at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:154-158 adds `if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(... expected a Verdict)` right after `verdict = confirm(...)` and before the `verdict.hypothesis_id` misattribution check. This correctly converts any non-Verdict confirmer return (e.g. None from a missing return) into a typed FirewallViolation instead of an opaque AttributeError. Idiomatic and consistent with the surrounding FirewallViolation guards and the documented #F-021 pipeline pattern.

Test test_discover_and_confirm_rejects_non_verdict_confirmer (tests/test_discovery.py:76) passes a confirmer returning None and asserts pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"). It DISCRIMINATES: I reverted the guard and the test failed with exactly `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` at the next line — the precise failure mode the fix addresses; restored cleanly (git diff clean). No regression: all 12 tests in test_discovery.py pass with the fix in place. Guard ordering is correct (type check precedes attribute access).

content.text: CONFIRMED. The G-011 fix is both appropriate and complete, and the regression test discriminates (fails with the exact opaque AttributeError when reverted).
